briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. We will now begin the climate briefing for March 15, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Climate Crisis: Threatening Daily Life and Ecosystems
sections.content: Today's news warns that climate change is profoundly impacting our lives. Globally, coffee production is decreasing due to floods and heatwaves [1], and domestically, honeybees have been directly hit by the climate crisis, prompting the government to accelerate the development of nectar-producing tree species [5]. Furthermore, significant changes are being detected across ecosystems, such as the advancement of penguin breeding seasons due to the climate crisis [6]. These changes remind us that the climate crisis is no longer a distant future concern but a present reality directly threatening our daily lives and nature.

sections.title: ⚠️ Carbon Neutrality Policy Discussions and the Importance of Social Consensus
sections.content: Policy initiatives for implementing carbon neutrality are also active. The government has taken the first step to enhance corporate carbon emission transparency by presenting practical standards for greenhouse gas disclosure [2]. However, opposition is spreading regarding the public discussion process for amending the Carbon Neutrality Framework Act, which includes 'convex-shaped' reduction measures [7, 26, 61], indicating the importance of achieving social consensus during policy implementation. These discussions demonstrate that in setting a concrete path toward achieving carbon neutrality, in-depth social dialogue and the participation of various stakeholders are essential.

sections.title: 🌱 Spreading Community-Led Carbon Neutrality Practices
sections.content: Across the nation, carbon neutrality efforts based on citizen participation are actively underway. Incheon City has designated the 15th of every month as '1.5°C Climate Action Day' and is expanding citizen-participatory carbon-neutral lifestyle practices [8, 13, 17, 19, 20, 30], while cities like Daejeon and Cheongju are recruiting participants for the vehicle carbon neutrality points system, offering incentives for reducing driving distances [9, 10, 12, 18, 21, 22]. Additionally, various local initiatives such as the Jeonnam Provincial Office of Education's selection of carbon-neutral leading schools [14], Namwon City's creation of an eco-healing environment [15], and Daedeok-gu's environmental guardian campaign [27] are contributing to achieving carbon neutrality goals. This voluntary and active participation from local communities is becoming a powerful driving force for the transition to a carbon-neutral society.

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Expansion and Infrastructure Challenges
sections.content: The government is accelerating the expansion of renewable energy, conditionally designating seven offshore wind power complex zones applied for by five local governments, including Incheon, Jeonnam, Jeonbuk, Boryeong in Chungnam, and Gunsan in Jeonbuk [4, 36, 38, 42, 43, 45, 47, 49, 51, 52, 53, 55, 56, 57, 59, 60, 62]. Notably, a large-scale power generation complex equivalent to 11 nuclear power plants, totaling 11GW, is planned for the West Coast alone [37, 40, 58], which is considered a green light for energy transition. Amid instability in the crude oil market due to the Middle East conflict, the importance of renewable energy is further highlighted, with Shinan County's renewable energy drawing attention [39, 50, 54]; however, behind this rapid push for renewable energy lies the realistic challenge of an insufficient power grid [41], underscoring the urgency of expanding infrastructure for a stable energy transition.

sections.title: 🌱 Future Response through Climate Change Adaptation and Industrial Innovation
sections.content: In response to the risk of infectious disease spread due to climate change, the Korea Disease Control and Prevention Agency (KDCA) has initiated surveillance activities for disease-carrying mosquitoes, strengthening proactive prevention efforts [3]. Innovation for carbon-neutral transition is also continuing in the industrial sector, with Hyundai Rotem seeking to transform beyond manufacturing into a 'carbon-neutral infrastructure company' [24], and pursuing the securing of a carbon-neutral 'golden time' through the 'Green Shaheen' project, which involves petrochemical advancement [31]. Furthermore, various approaches through technological innovation, such as AI-powered solar power plant management and power trading connections [44], and the presentation of carbon neutrality in public architecture through wooden construction [28], offer hope for future climate change response. These multifaceted efforts reflect our society's determination to adapt and create new opportunities amidst the threats of climate change.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has highlighted the severity of the climate crisis while also showcasing the diverse domestic and international efforts underway to overcome it. Moving forward, we must accelerate the transition to a carbon-neutral society through continuous policy implementation, citizen participation, and technological innovation. This concludes the climate briefing for March 15, 2026.

articles.title: What is 'Convex-Shaped' Reduction? Opposition Spreads Over Public Deliberation on Carbon Neutrality Framework Act
articles.original_title: ‘볼록형’ 감축이 뭐길래···탄소중립기본법 공론화 반발 확산
articles.description: An activist from the plaintiffs and legal teams of the Youth, Citizen, and Baby Climate Lawsuits, and the Carbon Neutrality Basic Plan Lawsuit, held a press conference on August 27th last year in front of the Statue of Admiral Yi Sun-sin in Gwanghwamun, Seoul. They stated, 'The rights guaranteed by the Constitution; the government and National Assembly must respond,' marking the 1st anniversary of the climate constitutional complaint decision. Senior Reporter Jeong Ji-yoon reports that the Deliberation Committee under the National Assembly's Special Committee on Climate Crisis is currently undergoing a public deliberation process for the amendment of the Carbon Neutrality and Green Growth Framework Act (Carbon Neutrality Framework Act)...

summary.phenomenon: On August 27th last year, in Gwanghwamun, Seoul, the plaintiffs and legal teams of the Youth, Citizen, and Baby Climate Lawsuits, and the Carbon Neutrality Basic Plan Lawsuit, held a press conference to mark the 1st anniversary of the climate constitutional complaint decision. They are currently protesting the public deliberation process for the amendment of the Carbon Neutrality and Green Growth Framework Act, which is being conducted by the Deliberation Committee under the National Assembly's Special Committee on Climate Crisis.

articles.original_description: 청소년·시민·아기기후소송과 탄소중립기본계획소송 청구인단 및 변호인단 소속 한 활동가가 지난해 8월 27일 서울 광화문 이순신 동상 앞에서 “헌법이 보장한 권리, 정부와 국회는 응답하라”며 기후 헌법소원 결정 1주년 기자회견을 하고 있다. 정지윤 선임기자국회 기후위기특별위원회 산하 공론화위원회가 탄소중립·녹색성장 기본법(탄소중립기본법) 개정 공론화 과정에서···

articles.title: Ministry of Climate, Energy, and Environment Conditionally Designates 7 Offshore Wind Power Complex Sites, Including Incheon and Jeonnam
articles.original_title: 기후부, 인천·전남 등 해상풍력 집적화단지 7곳 조건부 지정
articles.description: An image of an offshore wind turbine operating in Korea. Reporter Kang Yoon-jung. The Ministry of Climate, Energy, and Environment announced on the 15th that, following deliberation and resolution by the Renewable Energy Policy Deliberation Committee, it has conditionally designated 7 projects applied for by 5 local governments (Incheon, Jeonnam, Jeonbuk, Boryeong in Chungnam, and Gunsan in Jeonbuk) as renewable energy offshore wind power complexes. Renewable energy complexes are designed for local governments to lead in identifying power generation project sites, securing public acceptance and environmental suitability to promote renewable energy generation...

summary.phenomenon: On the 15th, the Ministry of Climate, Energy, and Environment conditionally designated 7 projects, applied for by 5 local governments including Incheon, Jeonnam, Jeonbuk, Boryeong in Chungnam, and Gunsan in Jeonbuk, as renewable energy offshore wind power complexes, following deliberation and resolution by the Renewable Energy Policy Deliberation Committee.
summary.cause: This designation is a policy decision aimed at promoting renewable energy development by enabling local governments to lead in identifying power generation project sites and securing public acceptance and environmental suitability.
summary.outlook: As a result, renewable energy generation is expected to expand, and offshore wind power development is projected to proceed more systematically through local government-led identification of power generation project sites and securing public acceptance.

keywords: Ministry of Climate, Energy, and Environment
articles.original_description: 국내에서 가동 중인 해상풍력 발전기 모습. 강윤중 기자기후에너지환경부는 재생에너지정책심의회의 심의·의결을 거쳐 5개 지자체(인천·전남·전북·충남 보령·전북 군산)가 신청한 7개 사업을 재생에너지 해상풍력 집적화단지로 조건부 지정한다고 15일 밝혔다.재생에너지 집적화단지는 지자체가 주도해 발전사업 입지를 발굴하고 주민 수용성과 환경성을 확보해 재생에너지 발전···
